Premier League Scores, Fixtures, Results, Tables & Stats, with LiveScore

The latest Premier League Live Scores, Results, Fixtures, Tables & Stats

Find Premier League scores, results, and fixtures instantly with LiveScore. See the live league table and get all the essential statistics for every team and player.

Today's Premier League Scores

LiveScore is a pioneer in delivering real-time, ultra-fast, and highly reliable Premier League scores and updates. Whether you’re checking scores today, following the latest games, or tracking the table and standings as they shift, this page keeps everything in one place. You can quickly follow live action, recent results, and key updates from England’s top flight without missing a moment.


About The Premier League and Its Format

The Premier League began in 1992 and features 20 participating clubs. Broadcast in over 200 countries, it is the most-watched football league globally and the richest, with prize money for champions and relegated teams exceeding £100 million. The league sits at the top of the English Football League pyramid, above the Championship, League One, and League Two. If teams finish with equal points, their position is determined by goal difference and then goals scored. Past winners include Manchester United, Blackburn Rovers, Arsenal, Chelsea, Leicester City, Liverpool, and Manchester City, with Manchester United and Manchester City noted for sustained title success.


Upcoming Premier League Fixtures

Upcoming Premier League fixtures highlight the next set of matches across the competition, with kick-off times spread over weekends and midweek rounds. Following the schedule makes it easier to track live scores, spot key head-to-head clashes, and understand how results in each gameweek may affect the league table as the season unfolds.


When Does the Premier League Season Start and End?

The 2025/26 Premier League season starts on August 16 and ends on May 24. Over these nine months, clubs balance league matches with cup competitions and international call-ups, so the calendar includes scheduled pauses.


Premier League Transfer Window Details

This summer period is when clubs reshape squads, add depth and address tactical needs before the season starts. This summer, the transfer window ran from June until September and reopened in the winter from the start of January to early - a great opportunity for clubs to address squad injury woes.


2025/2026 Season So Far

Arsenal had emerged as the standout performers in the 2025/26 Premier League season so far, opening up a clear lead at the top thanks to consistent results and one of the league’s strongest defensive records. Manchester City have clawed their way back into the title race and remain their closest challengers, while Manchester United and Aston Villa have also stayed in the hunt for Champions League places.


A major surprise has been Villa’s sustained push near the summit after an inconsistent start, highlighting tactical evolution under their manager. Meanwhile, traditional heavyweights such as Liverpool and Chelsea have struggled for consistency at times, leaving the top-four race tightly contested.


At the other end, the relegation battle has intensified, with several clubs - including Tottenham, West Ham and promoted sides - dragged into a tense survival fight as the season enters its decisive phase.


2025/2026 Top Scorers

The race for the Golden Boot is heating up, with a mix of established stars and new challengers in contention. Manchester City's **Erling Haaland** currently leads the way, continuing his prolific form from previous seasons.


He faces strong competition from Brentford's **Igor Thiago**, who has enjoyed an impressive campaign. Haaland's own teammate, **Antoine Semenyo**, is also high up in the scoring charts. The list of top contenders also includes:


  • **João Pedro** (Chelsea)

  • **Morgan Gibbs-White** (Nottingham Forest)

  • **Danny Welbeck** (Brighton)

  • **Viktor Gyökeres** (Arsenal)

  • **Eli Junior Kroupi** (AFC Bournemouth)

  • **Hugo Ekitike** (Liverpool)

  • **Ollie Watkins** (Aston Villa)

  • **Dominic Calvert-Lewin** (Leeds United)

See all Premier League stats

Promotion and Relegation System

Each Premier League season consists of 38 games. At the end of the schedule, the bottom three teams are relegated to the EFL Championship. The top two teams from the Championship earn automatic promotion, while the third promotion spot is decided through a playoff involving the teams finishing third to sixth. In short, the top two are promoted automatically, and the next four compete for a place in the Premier League via a playoff. See the latest Premier League table.


Participation in European and Domestic Cups

The fight near the top of the table is about more than the title. The top four positions guarantee UEFA Champions League qualification, so competition for those places is especially intense. Premier League clubs also participate in:


  • The FA Cup

  • The League Cup (EFL Cup)

  • The UEFA Champions League

European qualification remains one of the biggest targets for leading clubs, alongside domestic silverware and strong league finishes.

Albania Superliga 2025/2026 Standings, Fixtures, Results & Odds

The Albania Superliga is one of Eastern Europe’s most compelling football competitions — a league where title races are tight, results are rarely predictable, and the data behind each matchday tells a story worth reading. This page brings together everything that matters for anyone following the Albanian top flight: up-to-date standings, the full fixture schedule, match results, live scores, and odds across the most relevant markets. Whether you’re analyzing the Albania Superliga table to understand momentum shifts, reviewing head-to-head records before a big derby, or comparing Albania Superliga betting odds to find the sharpest line, the information here supports sharper, better-informed decisions.

The Albanian Superliga continues growing in visibility internationally, with increasing interest in the Albania League table, Albanian Football League standings, and broader Albanian Football development.

Competition Format of Albania Superliga

The Albania Superliga — officially the Kategoria Superiore — is governed by the Football Association of Albania (FSHF). Every club faces each opponent twice: once at home, once away. Three points for a win, one for a draw, zero for a defeat. Tiebreakers apply when clubs finish level on points, a detail that becomes critical in the final weeks.

Top finishers earn UEFA qualifying berths. The champion enters the Champions League path; other high-placed clubs access the Europa League or Conference League. Bottom clubs face relegation to the Kategoria e Parë, keeping pressure on throughout the season.

The Albania First Division and Superliga Albania structure together form the foundation of professional football in the country.

Albania Superliga Transfer Window

The Superliga operates two standard windows aligned with UEFA and FIFA regulations. The summer window closes at the end of August and drives the bulk of squad-building. The January window is used primarily for loans and squad adjustments.

FK Egnatia have been the most active club in the Albanian market recently, recruiting from the Balkans and South America to accelerate their rise. Their approach signals a broader shift — the transfer market in Albania is becoming more ambitious. FK Partizani and SK Tirana have also reinforced squads ahead of simultaneous UEFA qualifying commitments, adding depth across attacking positions.

Albanian Football Teams are increasingly scouting abroad while continuing to develop local talent capable of moving into larger European leagues.

Albania Superliga Results & Live Scores Today

The 2024/2025 season delivered clear statistical standouts. FK Egnatia and FK Partizani ranked among the most productive attacking sides, with Egnatia finishing among the top teams for total goals — a direct return on their investment in forward-line quality.

The individual golden boot went to a striker who surpassed 15 league goals, the benchmark for elite production in this division. The assists chart was led by a midfielder from one of the Tirana clubs, reinforcing how central the capital remains to the league’s statistical story. The title-winning side conceded fewer than 25 goals across the full campaign, while the highest-scoring team exceeded 55 — demonstrating the attacking ambition now defining the top half of the Albania Superliga table.

Fans closely tracking Albania score updates and Croatia-style Balkan football analytics continue paying greater attention to the Albanian Superliga standings.

Team Stadiums & Attendance

The Air Albania Stadium in Tirana holds approximately 22,000 seats and hosts SK Tirana and FK Partizani for major fixtures. It is the most modern venue in Albanian football. Loro Borici Stadium in Shkodër seats around 16,000 and is historically significant as FK Vllaznia’s home. FK Skënderbeu operate from an 8,000-capacity ground in Korçë.

Average attendance sits between 2,000 and 5,000 per fixture. Derby matches — particularly the Tirana derby — can fill the Air Albania Stadium close to capacity. Home sides in high-attendance Albania Superliga matches win at a measurably higher rate, a pattern consistent with crowd-influence research across smaller European leagues.

The atmosphere surrounding Albanian Football matches continues strengthening the identity of the Albania Championship and domestic league structure.

Where to Watch Albania Superliga & TV Partners

SuperSport Albania and RTSH carry live domestic coverage. For the Albanian diaspora, Tring TV is the primary subscription platform with the most reliable international access. Arena Sport networks cover selected fixtures across the Balkan region.

Bettors should check whether their bookmaker offers live streaming for Albania Superliga matches. Several major licensed platforms combine live visuals with real-time markets — a genuine edge for in-play betting.

Interest in Albania National Football Team standings has also increased overall attention toward the domestic Albanian Football League.

Albania Superliga Facts & Season Overview

The Superliga averages approximately 2.4 to 2.7 goals per match across a full season — mid-range for European leagues of its tier. Derby fixtures and motivationally asymmetric matches skew higher; cautious mid-table clashes frequently finish under 2.5 goals.

Squad values range from €3 million to €8 million per club, with FK Egnatia and FK Partizani holding the highest-valued rosters. Individual player values typically sit between €500,000 and €1.5 million. Despite modest coefficients, the league’s tactical variety and result unpredictability make Albania Superliga odds genuinely interesting for informed bettors.

The Albania Superliga and Albania League competitions continue evolving as the country invests further into football infrastructure and youth development.

Albania Superliga Odds & Betting Tips

The core markets available on Albania Superliga matches are match result (1X2), Asian handicap, over/under goals, both teams to score, and outright winner. Asian handicap is particularly useful in fixtures with a clear favorite. Goals markets reward bettors who track team-specific defensive and attacking stats rather than relying on assumptions.

Comparing Albania Superliga odds across at least two platforms before placing any bet remains the most effective way to protect long-term value.

Bookmakers and sponsors active in the Albania Superliga:

  • Betsson — shirt and stadium branding across multiple clubs
  • Unibet — active in the Albanian market with Superliga coverage
  • bet365 — full market depth including in-play and live streaming
  • Fortuna — present across the broader Balkan betting market
  • Meridianbet — strong regional presence in Albanian football sponsorship

FAQ

Which team is the favourite to win the Albania Superliga 2025/2026?

FK Partizani Tirana and SK Tirana enter the Albania Superliga table 2025/26 as analytical favorites based on squad depth and coaching experience. FK Egnatia are the most credible challenger, having invested consistently across multiple windows. Outright odds reflect this three-way dynamic — Partizani and Tirana price shortest, Egnatia offers value for bettors backing a title disruption.

Where to check Albania Superliga results and live scores today?

FlashScore and SofaScore provide real-time Albania Superliga live scores, lineups, and match statistics for every fixture. Most licensed bookmakers integrate live score trackers into their in-play interface. For in-play markets, having a live source open alongside your betting platform is the baseline for informed decisions.

What are the current odds to win the Albania Superliga?

Odds to win Albania Superliga betting shift throughout the season in response to results, injuries, and market demand. A club priced long in August may shorten significantly after a strong run of fixtures. The value lies in identifying clubs whose true title probability exceeds the implied probability in their current price — that gap is where informed bettors operate.
